title: cordylanthus pilosus subsp. diffusus
intro: Cordylanthus pilosus subsp. diffusus, also known as the Pacific Indian paintbrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of nearby plants, but also photosynthesizes like most other plants. The medicinal properties of Cordylanthus pilosus subsp. diffusus include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems, and is often consumed as a tea or infused in oil for topical application.
